revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Haginoo Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Haginoo Kofun | native_name =萩ノ尾古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Haginoo Kofun interior.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haginoo Kofun interior | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Haginoo Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 41 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|00|11|N|130|28|15|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|en|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|qid=Q385623|lt=Ōmuta, Fukuoka|1=Ōmuta, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|11|N|130|28|15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Haginoo Kofun'''|萩ノ尾古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Higashihagio neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|en|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|qid=Q385623|lt=Ōmuta|1=Ōmuta, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202866|title=萩ノ尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the only survivor of what was once known as the Shobara Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Haginoo Kofun is located at the western end of the Shobara Plateau, which extends to the west of Mount Yakeishi in the southern part of the city of Ōmuta, and is approximately 41 meters above sea level. To the south, the Suwa River, which forms the prefectural border between Fukuoka and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, flows westward, and {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}, which is a {{ill|Pyroclastic flow|en|Pyroclastic flow|qid=Q221616|lt=Pyroclastic flow|1=Pyroclastic flow}} deposit from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|qid=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}}, is widely distributed throughout the area, and tuff is also used for the stone materials of this tumulus.
It is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, with a diameter of 21 meters and a height of about five meters, with a side cave-style horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] facing west.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 2000, it was built around the end of the 6th century, based on excavated earthenware. The chamber is divided into a {{ill|Vestibule (architecture)|en|Vestibule (architecture)|qid=Q301276|lt=vestibule|1=Vestibule (architecture)}} and the burial chamber proper, and the burial chamber consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. Each chamber was constructed by placing huge cut monoliths of tuff as foundations at the bottom of the walls and stacking split stones at the top, and the ceilings are quite high. The posterior chamber has a floor width of approximately 2.67 meters, a length of approximately 2.9 meters, and a height of approximately 3 meters. There is a stone shelf on the back wall, and the wall below it has designs in red pigment. The designs can be broadly divided into two groups, and the group on the upper right shows two {{ill|gondola|en|gondola|qid=Q182916|lt=gondola|1=Gondola}}-shaped boats and a circular pattern with a dot in the center lined up one above the other. There is also an ellipse below the ship on the left. The group on the lower left has decorations that look like shields or bows, but the basic motifs are geometric patterns based on circular and triangular patterns. Documents indicate that there used to be decorations on the right wall of the burial chamber, but this cannot be confirmed today. The burial chamber has been opened for centuries, and an inscription dated 1692 was found carved into one of the stones.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The front room is smaller, with a floor width of approximately 1.66 meters, length of approximately 1.84 meters, and height of approximately 2.66 meter. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is notable for its burial chamber structure, and that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with paintings in a single color on the walls. It is located adjacent to the Ariake National College of Technology, <ref name="Shiseki"/> or 4.6 kilometers southeast of {{ill|Ōmuta Station|en|Ōmuta Station|qid=Q871063|lt=Ōmuta Station|1=Ōmuta Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Haginoo Kofun}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14896 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://one-plate.city.omuta.lg.jp/spots/detail/d1d5649c-181b-4144-90cb-88a359aac11f Omuta City Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} Haginoo Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}
